page: Microsoft Azure E4as_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Eav6 server family. It is powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at 3.7 GHz, featuring 2 physical cores and 4 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) via hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable. The CPU includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and SHA_NI.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance and parallel execution scale moderately, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the middle 50%. A notable bottleneck is memory bandwidth, where both small-block cached and large-block uncached read-only operations perform in the bottom 25%. This limitation is reflected in weaker multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation benchmarks. For machine learning workloads, the server handles small 135M parameter models with average efficiency but exhibits poor performance on medium 7B parameter models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E4as_v6 offers a dense memory profile relative to its compute footprint, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing or high memory read bandwidth. Given the 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server is well-suited for memory-optimized workloads such as caching layers, medium-sized databases, text processing, and general-purpose web serving. It is less suitable for data-intensive compilation, high-throughput database operations, or complex LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E4as_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Eav6 family, built on the x86_64 architecture. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz with 2 physical cores and 4 vCPUs. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8 GB per vCPU ratio, but lacks local storage. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. However, multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth are weaker, placing in the bottom 25%. This resource profile makes the E4as_v6 highly suitable for memory-intensive but compute-moderate workloads, such as caching, text processing, and web serving, while serving as a cost-efficient option where high memory density is required without the expense of additional CPU cores.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4as_v6 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 4 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor and 32 GB of RAM. It offers strong single-core performance but exhibits lower memory read bandwidth.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4as_v6 is an AMD-powered virtual machine with 4 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and high memory density for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 4 vCPUs and 3.7 GHz clock speed
32.0 GB of system memory providing an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
Strong single-core performance in CPU and text processing benchmarks
No local storage included, with support for Premium Storage
Best suited for memory-bound workloads, caching, and web serving
